Paper Title: R2GenGPT: Radiology Report Generation with Frozen LLMs
Key Method: Frozen LLM + Swin-T + Linear Visual Mapper (Shallow/Delta/Deep)
Notes: Proposed R2GenGPT, which aligns visual features with the word embedding space of LLMs using an efficient visual alignment module. Employs delta tuning to freeze the LLM entirely, serving as a direct and critical baseline for our QCXR-Flamingo architecture.

Paper Title: Enhanced Contrastive Learning with Multi-view Longitudinal Data for CXR Report Generation
Key Method: Multi-view contrastive learning with longitudinal data
Notes: Introduces MLRG to facilitate CXR report generation using enhanced contrastive learning with multi-view longitudinal data. Focuses on integrating spatial and temporal information to track disease progression.

Paper Title: FactCheXcker: Mitigating Measurement Hallucinations in CXR Report Generation
Notes: Mitigates measurement hallucinations in CXR report generation using a modular framework and code generation capabilities from LLMs. Employs a query-code-update paradigm to accurately extract measurable findings.

Paper Title: A Disease-Aware Dual-Stage Framework for Chest X-ray Report Generation
Notes: Introduces a dual-stage disease-aware framework. Stage 1 learns Disease-Aware Semantic Tokens (DASTs) via multi-label classification. Stage 2 uses a Disease-Visual Attention Fusion module to integrate features, significantly improving disease-awareness.

Paper Title: CXPMRG-Bench: Pre-training and Benchmarking for X-ray Medical Report Generation
Notes: Provides a comprehensive pre-training and benchmarking framework specifically tailored for X-ray medical report generation using the CheXpert Plus dataset, addressing the limitation of undocumented comparative evaluation baselines.

Paper Title: VILA-M3: Enhancing Vision-Language Models with Medical Expert Knowledge
Notes: Enhances generalist Vision-Language Models (like LLaVA) by introducing a specialized fourth phase of Instruction Fine-Tuning. It integrates domain expert models and medical expert knowledge to outperform standard generalist VLMs in healthcare applications.

Paper Title: MedRegA: Interpretable Bilingual Multimodal LLM for Diverse Biomedical Tasks
Notes: Proposes the first interpretable, bilingual, generalist MLLM that explicitly handles both image-level and region-level medical tasks. Employs MedRegInstruct dataset to enable detailed anatomical region grounding.

Paper Title: Learning Self-Critiquing Mechanisms for Region-Guided CXR Report Generation
Notes: Presents the RadSCR framework, which introduces a self-critiquing mechanism independent of costly LLMs. It end-to-end learns to identify and verify abnormality regions by comparing against alternative hypotheses and pseudo-negatives before generating the report.

Paper Title: Rethinking Radiology Report Generation: From Narrative Flow to Topic-Guided Findings
Notes: Argues against optimizing models for pure narrative flow, which causes over-reliance on linguistic priors instead of visual grounding. Introduces LLaVA-TA, which decomposes reports into anatomically-grounded independent topics to improve factual accuracy.

Paper Title: GEMeX: A Large-Scale Groundable and Explainable Medical VQA Benchmark
Notes: A large-scale benchmark for Medical VQA providing multi-modal explainability. Offers both textual and visual explanations for questions to enhance answer comprehensibility for diagnosis.

Paper Title: CURV: Coherent Uncertainty-Aware Reasoning in VLMs for X-Ray Report Generation
Notes: Also known computationally as RadZero. Implements VL-CABS (Vision-Language Cross-Attention Based on Similarity) to align text embeddings with local image features. Evaluates uncertainty-aware reasoning with zero-shot multi-task capability.

Paper Title: Radiology Report Generation via Multi-objective Preference Optimization
Notes: Utilizes multi-objective reinforcement learning to align RRG outputs with complex, heterogeneous radiologist preferences. Leverages multi-dimensional reward functions to balance clinical accuracy and sentence fluency.

Paper Title: HC-LLM: Historical-Constrained Large Language Models for Radiology Report Generation
Notes: A Historical-Constrained Large Language Model platform. Integrates historical patient data and longitudinal image comparisons into the reporting workflow to significantly improve follow-up examination analysis.

Paper Title: LLM-RG4: Flexible and Factual Radiology Report Generation across Diverse Input Contexts
Key Method: LLM instruction-following for diverse clinical contexts
Notes: Focuses on input diversity rather than single static benchmarks. Employs an adaptive token fusion module and uses the new MIMIC-RG4 dataset to handle complex real-world instruction-following contexts where varied information is available.

Paper Title: DAMPER: Dual-Stage Medical Report Generation with MeSH Alignment
Key Method: Coarse MeSH alignment + fine hypergraph matching
Notes: A dual-stage framework that simulates radiologist reading patterns. Utilizes MeSH-guided coarse-grained alignment and hypergraph-enhanced fine-grained node matching to reduce semantic fragmentation.

Paper Title: PriorRG: Prior-Guided Contrastive Pre-training and Coarse-to-Fine Decoding
Notes: A two-stage training pipeline focusing heavily on extracting prior patient clinical context. Uses prior-guided contrastive pre-training to establish spatiotemporal feature extraction aligned with real-world contextual radiologist workflows.

Paper Title: MedRAX: Medical Reasoning Agent for Chest X-ray
Notes: Introduces a versatile AI agent combining state-of-the-art CXR analysis pipelines with an MLLM framework. Dynamically evaluates complex medical queries using the comprehensive ChestAgentBench testing suite.

Paper Title: SEI: Structural Entities Extraction and Patient Indications Incorporation for CXR
Notes: Implements Structural Entity Extraction (SEE) to suppress noisy presentation-style vocabulary. Incorporates patient indications to boost zero-shot cross-modal attention generation capabilities.

Paper Title: CXRL: Text-Driven CXR Generation with Reinforcement Learning
Key Method: RL with comparative feedback for CXR generation
Notes: Targets text-to-image Generation (rather than Image-to-Text). Uses reinforcement learning with comparative feedback (RLCF) across domain-specific reward models to control physiological constraints in the generated CXRs.

Paper Title: TRRG: Truthful Radiology Report Generation with Cross-Modal Disease Clue Enhanced LLMs
Notes: Truthful Radiology Report Generation approach. Employs cross-modal strategies bridging identified disease clusters to prompt the underlying generative LLM safely.

Paper Title: Semantic-Aware CXR Report Generation with Domain-Specific Lexicon
Notes: Approaches the semantic disconnect between generic text generation and specific medical dictionaries. Focuses on constrained lexical retrieval during generation decoding steps.

Paper Title: EKAGen: Instance-level Expert Knowledge and Aggregate Discriminative Attention
Notes: Injects instance-level expert knowledge via discriminative attention modules, filtering generic image patches and prioritizing semantically dense diagnostic areas.

Paper Title: InVERGe: Intelligent Visual Encoder for Bridging Modalities in Report Generation
Notes: Integrates a Cross-Modal Query Fusion Layer (CMQFL) to intelligently bridge visual encoder outputs directly with generative linguistic structures without heavy catastrophic forgetting.

Paper Title: R2Gen-Mamba: Selective State Space Model for Radiology Report Generation
Notes: The first paper integrating the Selective State Space Model (Mamba) into RRG to dramatically cut computational training complexity while preserving linear long-range context dependencies historically monopolized by Transformers.

Paper Title: RADAR: Enhancing Radiology Report Generation with Supplementary Knowledge Injection
Notes: Combines inherent LLM parameters with externally retrieved clinical guidelines (RAG architecture). Systematically filters repetitive injection via a supplementary knowledge validation stage.

Paper Title: Libra: Leveraging Temporal Images for Biomedical Radiology Analysis
Notes: Builds a Temporal Alignment Connector (TAC) specifically to encode comparative differences between current and historical prior-images. Vastly outperforms single-event static modeling on longitudinal metrics.

Paper Title: CheXagent: Towards a Foundation Model for Chest X-Ray Interpretation
Notes: Major foundation model standard developed at Stanford. Achieves state-of-the-art multi-task performance by interpreting highly detailed chest x-ray semantics through pure foundational scale. Must be reviewed carefully.

Paper Title: XrayGPT: Chest Radiographs Summarization using Large Medical VLMs
Notes: A massive vision-language conversational agent utilizing MedClip as the visual encoder linearly projected into a Vicuna LLM, specifically fine-tuned on 217k detailed, high-quality interactive radiograph scenarios.

Paper Title: BioBridge: Bridging Biomedical Foundation Models via Knowledge Graphs
Key Method: Knowledge graph bridging for biomedical models
Notes: Unifies completely distinct unimodal biomedical foundation models via an abstract structural Knowledge Graph. Avoids fine-tuning and instead uses the graph to calculate translational vectors across data silos.

Paper Title: RRG-Mamba: Efficient Radiology Report Generation with State Space Model
Notes: Further validates Mamba for automated radiology report generation. Replaces standard fixed Mamba embeddings with Rotary Position Encodings (RoPE) to effectively model complex spatial geometries in vision data.

Paper Title: STREAM: Spatio-Temporal and Retrieval-Augmented Modeling for CXR
Notes: Verified STREAM generally focuses on spatial-temporal tracking coupled with retrieval-augmented modeling for time-series CXR progression.

Paper Title: X-WIN: Building Chest Radiograph World Model via Predictive Sensing
Notes: Constructs a World Model that distills 3D volumetric intelligence from CT scans directly into 2D Chest X-Ray parameters, radically strengthening diagnostic awareness of overlapping anatomical structures.

Paper Title: CoGaze: Context- and Gaze-Guided Vision-Language Pretraining for CXR
Notes: Models radiologist visual gaze-tracking mechanisms alongside context-infused clinical intents. Implements multi-level semantic alignment through contrastive pre-training to faithfully mimic biological radiologist reading workflows.

Paper Title: HKRG: Hierarchical Knowledge Integration for Radiology Report Generation
Notes: Focuses on multi-level mapping strategies, taking generalized expert knowledge libraries and injecting them hierarchically into specific visual feature extractions over the MIMIC dataset.

Paper Title: CheXOne: A Reasoning-Enabled Vision-Language Foundation Model for CXR Interpretation
Notes: Reasoning-enabled Foundation Model. Overcomes the black box prediction failure of previous models by explicitly outputting visual reasoning chains parallel to language outputs, enabling high-trust reporting.

Paper Title: CMCRL: Cross-Modal Causal Representation Learning for Radiology Report Generation
Notes: Medical Phrase Grounding (MRG) using uMedGround via Cross-Modal Causal alignment. Embeds specific <BOX> tokens to mathematically lock text generations against distinct causal boundary boxes in the physical image.
